summary: Added a portable `SDATA` macro in `src/placeholder.h` so C declarations can force placement into the `.sdata` section under Metrowerks, enabling small string literals previously kept in assembly stubs to move into C. The PR migrated `.sdata` strings for `ftCo_09CB`, `ftafterimage`, and `ftcolanim` from deleted asm files into their corresponding C files, removed those asm objects from `obj_files.mk`, and adjusted an `OSThread_data.s` `.skip` size. The stated motivation was that this allows small strings to be migrated to C and should make it possible to migrate anything from asm to C.
what_changed: Introduced `SDATA` in `src/placeholder.h`, expanding to `__declspec(section ".sdata")` when building with `__MWERKS__` and not `M2CTX`, and to nothing otherwise.

Changed C string declarations that needed small-data placement from `extern char*` placeholders to real arrays annotated with `SDATA`, such as `ftCo_804D3B28[] = "jobj.h"`, `ftCo_804D3B30[] = "jobj"`, `ftCo_804D3B38[] = "scale"`, and `ftCo_804D3B40[] = "0"` in `ftCo_09CB.c`.
Moved `ftCo_804D3C30[] = "0"` into `src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c` as an `SDATA` string and removed its extern declaration from `ftafterimage.h`.
Moved `ftCo_803C6AE8[] = "don't reset spycloak colanim!\n"`, `ftCo_803C6B08[] = "ftcolanimlist.c"`, and `SDATA char ftCo_804D3C10[] = "0"` into `src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c`.
Deleted the former asm-only data files `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.s`, `asm/melee/ft/ftafterimage.s`, and `asm/melee/ft/ftcolanim.s` after their data was represented in C.
Removed the corresponding deleted asm object files from `TEXT_O_FILES` in `obj_files.mk`.
smart_moves: Used a macro rather than sprinkling raw Metrowerks-specific `__declspec(section ".sdata")` attributes across source files, keeping section-forcing readable and centralized.
Made `SDATA` compile away outside the relevant Metrowerks non-`M2CTX` configuration, preserving compatibility for analysis/context tooling and nonmatching builds.
Converted asm string labels into named C arrays with the same symbol names, preserving symbol identity while allowing object files to be removed from the assembly side.
Kept the existing `/* literal */` annotation style while adding `SDATA`, making the intent clear: these are literal data symbols whose exact section matters.
Removed obsolete extern declarations once the literal storage lived in the C translation unit, reducing header clutter and avoiding misleading pointer declarations for array-backed strings.
decomp_lessons: When small strings must land in `.sdata`, represent them in C as arrays annotated with a section-forcing macro instead of leaving a tiny companion `.s` file behind.
For decomp cleanup, a section macro can unlock migration of data-only asm stubs without changing call sites or symbol names.
Prefer `char symbol[] = "..."` for migrated string storage rather than `extern char*`, because the assembly labels represented storage for bytes, not pointer variables.
Centralize compiler-specific attributes in shared headers such as `placeholder.h` so future migrations can reuse the same mechanism safely.
After migrating asm data into C, update `obj_files.mk` in the same change to remove the old asm object and prevent duplicate symbols or stale build inputs.
Guard decomp-only matching helpers against `M2CTX` or non-Metrowerks contexts when the syntax is compiler-specific.
naming_conventions: The new macro is named `SDATA`, all caps like other low-level decomp helper macros such as `LITERAL`.
Migrated anonymous data labels kept their existing address-style names, for example `ftCo_804D3B28`, `ftCo_804D3C30`, and `ftCo_804D3C10`.
String literals retained the `/* literal */` source comment convention already used in the surrounding files.
The PR used `SDATA char name[] = "..."` for small-data strings rather than changing symbols to more semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `__declspec(section ".sdata")` under Metrowerks to force a C object into the `.sdata` section when natural compiler placement is insufficient.
Define the section-forcing macro as empty outside the exact compiler context to avoid breaking m2ctx or other tooling.

Replace small `.sdata` asm blocks containing `.asciz` strings and `.balign` directives with C `char[]` definitions annotated by `SDATA` when symbol names and section placement are enough to preserve layout.
Deleting asm data files and their `obj_files.mk` entries can be a safe matching step once equivalent C symbols occupy the required section.

The diff suggests `.sdata` string migration may require auditing nearby small-data reservation sizes, as shown by the `Reschedule` `.skip` adjustment from `0x8` to `0x4`; the exact rationale is not explained in the PR text.
follow_up_queries: Find later uses of `SDATA` to see which asm data stubs were unlocked by this macro.
Search for remaining `.sdata` asm files containing only `.asciz` strings that could be migrated to C with `SDATA`.
Audit `extern char*` declarations for literal labels that should be `char[]` definitions or `extern char[]` declarations.
Investigate why `Reschedule` in `OSThread_data.s` changed from `.skip 0x8` to `.skip 0x4` in the same PR.
